description: Element-wise and rolling-window mathematical utility functions operating on price series — arithmetic operators, period extrema, rounding, logarithms, exponentials, and trigonometric transforms

fields.name: real0
fields.label: First input series (required for binary operators ADD, SUB, MULT, DIV and all single-array functions)

fields.name: real1
fields.label: Second input series (required for binary operators ADD, SUB, MULT, DIV only)

fields.name: time_period
fields.label: Rolling window period (required for MAX, MAXINDEX, MIN, MININDEX, MINMAX, MINMAXINDEX, SUM; default 30; range 2-100000)

fields.name: out_values
fields.label: Primary output array (arithmetic, transform, extremum values)

fields.name: out_secondary
fields.label: Secondary output (MINMAX min array; MINMAXINDEX min-index array)

binary_operators.output_length: Same as input length (1-to-1 bar mapping)
binary_operators.edge_case: DIV: when real1[i] = 0, output is implementation-defined (typically 0 or ±Inf); caller must guard

formulas.MAX: Maximum value of real0 over the last timePeriod bars
formulas.MAXINDEX: Index of the bar holding the maximum value over timePeriod
formulas.MIN: Minimum value of real0 over the last timePeriod bars
formulas.MININDEX: Index of the bar holding the minimum value over timePeriod
formulas.MINMAX: Both MAX and MIN in one call (out_values = max array, out_secondary = min array)
formulas.MINMAXINDEX: Both MAXINDEX and MININDEX in one call (out_values = maxindex, out_secondary = minindex)

period_extrema.note: MAXINDEX / MININDEX output absolute bar indices relative to the input array (not relative to the window)

rolling_sum.use_case: Building block for manual SMA; cumulative position tracking; volume totals over period

formulas.ACOS: arccos(real0[i]); domain: -1 to +1; output: 0 to π

use_cases.price_cycles: SIN/COS applied to normalized price for cycle analysis
use_cases.angular_conversion: Convert LINEARREG_ANGLE output (degrees) to radians before applying trig functions

given: function_type in [ADD, SUB, MULT, DIV, CEIL, FLOOR, SQRT, LN, LOG10, EXP, SIN, COS, TAN, ASIN, ACOS, ATAN, SINH, COSH, TANH]
for binary operators: real1 present and same length as real0
compute_success_zero_lookback.result: Full-length output array aligned 1-to-1 with input; no lookback offset

given: function_type in [MAX, MAXINDEX, MIN, MININDEX, MINMAX, MINMAXINDEX, SUM]
compute_success_period_based.result: Rolling output offset by timePeriod-1 bars; use outBegIdx to align with source data

minmax_computed.result: Two parallel output arrays — extremum pair computed in one pass (more efficient than two separate calls)

any: function_type in [LN, LOG10] and any real0[i] <= 0
function_type in [ASIN, ACOS] and any real0[i] outside [-1, 1]

domain_error.result: Affected bars produce NaN or ±Inf; caller must validate domain constraints before calling

given: function_type in [MAX, MAXINDEX, MIN, MININDEX, MINMAX, MINMAXINDEX, SUM]
insufficient_data.result: No output; provide at least timePeriod bars

given: real0 and real1 differ in length
mismatched_arrays.result: Binary operators require equal-length input arrays

errors.message: Not enough bars for rolling window operation. Provide at least timePeriod bars.
Input domain violation (e.g., negative input to SQRT, zero denominator in DIV, out-of-range input to ASIN/ACOS).

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply LN or LOG10 to raw price series without checking for zero or negative values
anti_patterns.why: Price data that includes zero (e.g., instrument halted, data errors) produces -Inf; pre-filter or clamp to a small positive epsilon before logging

anti_patterns.rule: Do not pass degree values directly to SIN, COS, TAN without converting to radians
anti_patterns.why: All TA-Lib trig functions expect radians; LINEARREG_ANGLE outputs degrees — multiply by π/180 before passing to trig functions

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use DIV without guarding for zero denominators
anti_patterns.why: Integer or flat-price bars may produce a zero denominator; the C implementation propagates NaN or ±Inf silently — callers must validate

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use MAXINDEX / MININDEX as direct bar date references without mapping through outBegIdx
anti_patterns.why: The index returned is relative to the input array, not the original date array; if the caller sliced the input, the index is off by the slice offset

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use SUM as a substitute for SMA — divide by timePeriod to normalize
anti_patterns.why: SUM returns a raw total; SMA = SUM / timePeriod — using the raw SUM in a threshold comparison produces results that scale with both price and period length

lookback_summary.zero_lookback: ADD, SUB, MULT, DIV, CEIL, FLOOR, SQRT, LN, LOG10, EXP, SIN, COS, TAN, ASIN, ACOS, ATAN, SINH, COSH, TANH (19 functions)
lookback_summary.period_based: MAX, MAXINDEX, MIN, MININDEX, MINMAX, MINMAXINDEX, SUM (7 functions; lookback = timePeriod - 1)

extensions.output_type: Double Array for all except MAXINDEX/MININDEX/MINMAXINDEX which return Integer Arrays
